mission.title: open-policy-agent-opa-: Add support for rule-level tracing
mission.description: Add support for rule-level tracing. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-policy-agent-opa- deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-policy-agent-opa- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
helm list -n open-policy-agent-opa- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-policy-agent-opa- installation.

steps.title: Review open-policy-agent-opa- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-policy-agent-opa- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get configmap -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-policy-agent-opa-
```
It would be nice to have a cheap, efficient rule-level tracer that could be enabled in more situations than the current tracing implementation allows.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add support for rule-level tracing
steps.description:
Rules can now be annotated with a metadata `id` field. When `decision_logs.evaluated_rules` is enabled in the configuration, the IDs of successfully evaluated rules are included in decision log events. Additionally, the Data API supports a `?rules` query parameter to include evaluated rule IDs directly in the response payload.

```rego
# METADATA
# id: allow-admin
allow if input.role == "admin"
```

Modules containing `id` annotations will have metadata parsing enabled automatically.

----
I'll

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get events -n open-policy-agent-opa- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add support for rule-level tracing" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Rules can now be annotated with a metadata `id` field. When `decision_logs.evaluated_rules` is enabled in the configuration, the IDs of successfully evaluated rules are included in decision log events. Additionally, the Data API supports a `?rules` query parameter to include evaluated rule IDs directly in the response payload.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-policy-agent-opa- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
